string.name: object_info_sun_description
string.translation_description: Short description of the Sun

resources.string: The Sun is the star at the centre of our solar system. It provides the light and heat that makes life on Earth possible.

string.name: object_info_sun_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about the Sun

resources.string: The Sun contains 99.86% of all the mass in our solar system. About 1.3 million Earths could fit inside it!

string.name: object_info_procyon_description
string.translation_description: Short description of Procyon

resources.string: Procyon is a bright star in the constellation Canis Minor (the Lesser Dog). It\'s one of our closer stellar neighbours.

string.name: object_info_arcturus_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Arcturus

resources.string: Arcturus is travelling through space at a tremendous speed and will pass closest to our solar system in about 4,000 years!

string.name: object_info_aldebaran_description
string.translation_description: Short description of Aldebaran

resources.string: Aldebaran is an orange giant star that represents the eye of Taurus (the Bull). Its name means "the follower" in Arabic.

string.name: object_info_antares_description
string.translation_description: Short description of Antares

resources.string: Antares is a red supergiant star, the heart of Scorpius (the Scorpion). Its name means "rival of Mars" due to its red colour.

string.name: object_info_fomalhaut_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Fomalhaut shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Fomalhaut has a spectacular debris disc that was one of the first to be directly imaged around another star!

string.name: object_info_markab_description
string.translation_description: Description of Markab shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Markab is one of the four stars forming the Great Square of Pegasus, an easily recognisable asterism.

string.name: object_info_scheat_description
string.translation_description: Description of Scheat shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Scheat is a red giant star in the Great Square of Pegasus, notable for its deep orange-red colour.

string.name: object_info_m13_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M13 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: In 1974, the Arecibo message was beamed towards M13 as an attempt to contact extraterrestrial intelligence!

string.name: object_info_m45_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M45 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Pleiades are amongst the nearest star clusters to Earth and have been recognised by virtually every ancient civilisation!

string.name: object_info_m51_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M51 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Whirlpool was the first galaxy to be recognised as having spiral structure, discovered by Lord Rosse in 1845!

string.name: object_info_m81_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M81 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M81 is gravitationally interacting with neighbouring galaxy M82, triggering intense star formation in M82!

string.name: object_info_m82_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M82 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M82 is forming stars 10 times faster than our Milky Way due to gravitational interaction with neighbouring M81!

string.name: object_info_m104_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M104 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Sombrero Galaxy has a supermassive black hole at its centre estimated to be 1 billion solar masses!

string.name: object_info_m78_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M78 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M78 contains about 45 young T Tauri variable stars, very young stars still contracting towards the main sequence!

string.name: object_info_m22_description
string.translation_description: Description of M22 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M22 is one of the nearest and brightest globular clusters to Earth, located in Sagittarius near the galactic centre.

string.name: object_info_m2_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M2 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M2 contains about 150,000 stars and has a diameter of 175 light-years, making it one of the largest globular clusters!

string.name: object_info_m14_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M14 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: In 1938, a nova appeared in M14 but wasn\'t discovered until 1964 when old photographic plates were examined!

string.name: object_info_m53_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M53 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M53 is one of the most distant globular clusters from the galactic centre at 60,000 light-years away from it!

string.name: object_info_m66_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M66 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M66\'s asymmetric appearance is caused by gravitational interactions with its neighbours M65 and NGC 3628!

string.name: object_info_m77_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M77 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M77 harbours an active galactic nucleus with a black hole 15 million times the mass of our Sun!

string.name: object_info_m110_description
string.translation_description: Description of M110 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M110 is a dwarf elliptical galaxy and satellite of the Andromeda Galaxy, the last object added to the Messier catalogue.

string.name: object_info_m110_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M110 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M110 was actually observed by Messier in 1773 but not added to his catalogue until 1966!

string.name: object_info_orion_description
string.translation_description: Description of Orion shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Orion the Hunter is one of the most recognisable constellations, visible from both hemispheres with its distinctive Belt of three stars.

string.name: object_info_ursa_major_description
string.translation_description: Description of Ursa Major shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Ursa Major (the Great Bear) contains the Big Dipper asterism, one of the most recognised star patterns in the northern sky.

string.name: object_info_capricornus_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Capricornus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Capricornus is one of the oldest recognised constellations, appearing in Babylonian star catalogues over 3,000 years ago!

string.name: object_info_sagittarius_description
string.translation_description: Description of Sagittarius shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Sagittarius the Archer points towards the centre of our Milky Way galaxy, making it rich in nebulae and star clusters.

string.name: object_info_sagittarius_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Sagittarius shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: When you look at Sagittarius, you\'re looking toward Sagittarius A*, the supermassive black hole at our galaxy\'s centre!

string.name: object_info_aquila_description
string.translation_description: Description of Aquila shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Aquila the Eagle is a constellation on the celestial equator, containing the bright star Altair at its centre.

string.name: object_info_cepheus_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Cepheus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Cepheid variables discovered by Henrietta Leavitt revolutionised astronomy by allowing us to measure cosmic distances!

string.name: object_info_hercules_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Hercules shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Our solar system is moving towards a point in Hercules called the solar apex at about 20 km/s!

string.name: object_info_bootes_description
string.translation_description: Description of Bootes shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Boötes the Herdsman is a constellation containing Arcturus, the fourth brightest star in the night sky.

string.name: object_info_puppis_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Puppis shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Puppis contains M47, an open cluster visible to the naked eye that was \"lost\" for over 200 years due to a cataloguing error!
